data.title: Prosperity Bancshares Non-GAAP EPS of $1.50 beats by $0.06, revenue of $367.62M beats by $6.71M
data.description:
• Prosperity Bancshares press release (PB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.50 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $367.62M (+19.9% Y/Y) beats by $6.71M.
  • First quarter net interest margin increased 21 basis points to 3.51% compared to 3.30% for fourth quarter 2025
  • Loans, excluding Warehouse Purchase Program loans, increased $3.354 billion or 16.4% during first quarter 2026
  • Deposits increased $4.150 billion or 14.6% during first quarter 2026
  • Allowance for credit losses on loans and on off-balance sheet credit exposure of $421.5 million and allowance for credit losses on loans to total loans, excluding Warehouse Purchase Program, of 1.61%(1)
  • Nonperforming assets remain low at 0.33% of first quarter average interest-earning assets

data.title: Central Pacific Financial misses Q1 topline
data.description:
• Central Pacific Financial press release (CPF): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.78 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $70.6M (+2.6% Y/Y) misses by $3.4M.
  • Net income of $20.7 million, or $0.78 per diluted share
  • Return on average assets of 1.12% and return on average equity of 13.90%
  • Net interest margin of 3.53% and efficiency ratio of 59.87%
  • Total loans of $5.32 billion, increased by $31.3 million from the prior quarter
  • Total deposits of $6.70 billion, increased by $89.6 million from the prior quarter
  • Repurchased 321,396 shares of common stock at a total cost of $10.5 million during the quarter

data.title: Brinker International gains after another strong quarter for the Chili's chain
data.description:
Brinker International (EAT) traded higher in early action on Wednesday after releasing its fiscal third-quarter earnings report.

Comparable restaurant sales increased 3.3% in the quarter, including 4.0% for Chili's. Of note, Chili's comparable restaurant sales for February and March both increased 5.9% with positive traffic, while Chili's January comparable restaurant sales of 0.6% were adversely impacted by Winter Storm Fern and one fewer operating day resulting from a holiday shift. The Chili's chain delivered its 20th consecutive quarter of same-store sales growth and lapped a tough comparison to the 31% increase a year ago.

"Chili's continued strong performance is driven by a disciplined strategy focused on improving the fundamentals of food, service, and atmosphere, supported by ongoing menu innovation, everyday value, and attention-capturing media and advertising that reinforce the company's value proposition, drive trial among new guests, and strengthen loyalty," highlighted the company.

Restaurant operating margin as a percentage of sales slipped 50 basis points to 18.4%. Adjusted EBITDA of $223.7M was above last year's tally of $220.6M. Non-GAAP EPS of $2.90 beat the consensus estimate by $0.04 and was above last year's mark of $2.66.

Brinker International (EAT) sees FY26 revenue of $5.78B to $5.82B (midpoint $5.80B) and EPS of $10.45 to $10.85 (midpoint $10.65) vs. $10.69 consensus.

Shares of EAT were up 1.8% in premarket action to $131.40 vs. the 52-week range of $100.30 to $187.12. Short interest on the restaurant stock stands at 10.6% of the total float.

data.title: GSK declares $0.44/ADS or 17p/share dividend
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) declares approx. $0.44 per ADS or 17.00p/share quarterly dividend, -5.6% decrease from prior dividend of 18.00p, bringing total dividend declared to 70p/share for full year 2026.
  • Forward yield 124.84%
  • Payable July 9; for shareholders of record May 15; ex-div May 15.
  • Furthermore, £1.7 billion executed to date as part of the £2 billion share buyback programme announced at FY 2024.
  • See GSK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Alaska Airlines opens its European era with Seattle-to-Rome flight
data.description:
Alaska Airlines (ALK) entered a new Europe phase on April 28 with the launch of its first flight to Europe. The nonstop flight between Seattle-Tacoma International Airport and Rome Fiumicino also became the carrier's first scheduled direct link between Seattle and Rome. 

The route is operated with a Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner carrying Alaska’s new global livery and 34 international business-class suites, with the carrier emphasizing multi-course dining, upgraded bedding, amenity kits, and a broader premium long-haul service package. The company also described the flight as a cargo milestone, saying it became the first U.S. passenger airline to offer daily nonstop cargo service between Seattle and Rome, expanding its cargo network to 109 destinations worldwide.

Strategically, the Rome launch marks Alaska Airlines' (ALK) formal entry into Europe and was called a significant step in the company’s long-term growth strategy. Looking ahead, Alaska Airlines (ALK) plans to add Seattle-London Heathrow daily year-round service on May 21 and Seattle-Reykjavik daily summer seasonal service on May 28.

data.title: GE HealthCare Technologies Non-GAAP EPS of $0.99 misses by $0.06, revenue of $5.13B beats by $100M
data.description:
• GE HealthCare Technologies press release (GEHC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.99 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $5.13B (+7.3% Y/Y) beats by $100M.
  • Total orders up 1.1% organically versus 10.3% growth in the year-ago period; book-to-bill of 1.07 times and backlog of $21.8 billion

  • For the full-year 2026, guidance is as follows:

    • Organic revenue growth* of 3.0% to 4.0% year-over-year; unchanged
    • Adjusted EBIT margin* of 15.4% to 15.7%, reflecting an expansion of 10 bps to 40 bps year-over-year; this compares to previous Adjusted EBIT margin* guidance of 15.8% to 16.1%
    • Adjusted effective tax rate (ETR)* in the range of 20.0% to 21.0%; unchanged
    • Adjusted EPS* in the range of $4.80 to $5.00 vs. $5.06 consensus, representing 4.6% to 9.0% growth year-over-year; this compares to previous Adjusted EPS* guidance in the range of $4.95 to $5.15
    • Free cash flow* of approximately $1.6 billion, in-line with profit outlook; this compares to previous guidance of approximately $1.7 billion
